notes: v2 — hard-to-surface UI bugs. NONE emit a log; each is a subtle UI-STATE defect that only appears after a specific multi-step sequence and is detectable only by reading the accessibility tree (a Text's content is its AXLabel; a TextField's typed contents are its AXValue). Scored by the post-sequence a11y oracle (scripts/verify_ios_bugs.py) and/or semantic finding-match — there is no greppable log_signature.

bugs.title: Committed profile edits lost on re-entry; saved-summary contradicts the empty field
bugs.description: Profile's editable fields are fresh view-local @State never seeded back from the model (the .onAppear read-back is missing). After Continue commits a name, re-entering Profile shows the model-backed 'Saved profile' row with the name, but the Display name field is blank — a same-screen contradiction.
trigger: On Profile, type 'Wesley' into Display name and 'w@x.com' into Email
Tap Back once to land on the duplicate Profile
Read the 'Saved profile' row AND the Display name field
bugs.expected: On re-entry the Display name field is re-seeded from the model and reads 'Wesley', matching the saved summary.
bugs.actual: The 'Saved profile' row reads 'Wesley' but the Display name field is empty — the committed edit appears lost.
bugs.observable_ui_diff: profile.savedName AXLabel=='Wesley' while the Display name TextField AXValue is empty.
bugs.oracle: PRESENT iff a node AXLabel=='Wesley' exists AND the Display name field AXValue is empty; FIXED iff both are 'Wesley'.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports the Profile display-name field went blank after navigating away and back, although a saved name is still shown.

bugs.title: Save Int-normalizes the name and silently drops leading zeros while 'Saved' claims success
bugs.description: Save round-trips the name through Int to 'normalize' numeric input, so '007' becomes '7'. The green 'Saved' confirmation still appears, but the field (bound to the same @Published value) silently re-renders to '7'.
trigger: Re-read the 'Your name' field value and the 'Saved' confirmation
bugs.expected: The name persists verbatim ('007') and 'Saved' appears.
bugs.actual: The field silently becomes '7' while 'Saved' still appears — success is reported but the value changed.
bugs.observable_ui_diff: settings.name.field AXValue becomes '7' (not '007') after Save while settings.savedConfirmation reads 'Saved'.
bugs.oracle: PRESENT iff after typing '007' and Save the field AXValue=='7' AND a 'Saved' confirmation shows; FIXED iff AXValue=='007'.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports saving a value like '007' silently changes it (leading zeros dropped) though it claims success.

bugs.title: Character counter is off-by-one (boundary-safe: correct when empty, wrong once you type)
bugs.description: The 'Your name' counter renders max(0, count-1)/30, so the empty field shows a correct-looking '0/30' but every non-empty value is undercounted by one — 'Alice' (5) shows '4/30'.
trigger: On Settings, observe the counter reads '0/30' on the empty field
Read the counter and compare to the typed length
bugs.expected: The counter shows '5/30' for a 5-character name.
bugs.actual: The counter shows '4/30' — undercounts by one.
bugs.oracle: PRESENT iff after typing an N-char value the counter leading int == N-1; FIXED iff == N.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports the character counter is off by one / undercounts the typed length.

bugs.title: Profile completeness percent truncates instead of rounding (2 of 3 shows 66%, not 67%)
bugs.description: 'Profile completeness' is filled*100/3 with integer division, so two of three fields filled shows '66%' instead of round(66.67)=='67%'.
trigger: On Profile, fill exactly two of the three fields (Display name + Email), leave Phone empty
bugs.expected: Two of three fields rounds to '67%'.
bugs.oracle: PRESENT iff with 2 of 3 fields filled the percent == 66; FIXED iff == 67.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports the completeness percentage rounds down / is wrong (66% for two of three).

bugs.title: Continue pushes a duplicate Profile; the back stack is one screen too deep
bugs.description: Profile's Continue pushes a stray duplicate Profile before About (stack: Profile > Profile > About). Backing out of About lands on a blank Profile and two Backs never reach the Settings root.
trigger: On Profile, type a name and Email, tap Continue (lands on About)
Tap Back twice and read the navigation title
bugs.expected: Two Backs from About reach the Settings root (no Back button).
bugs.actual: After two Backs the title is still 'Profile' with a Back button present — the stack is one too deep.
bugs.observable_ui_diff: After two Backs the navigation-title is 'Profile' with a Back button; correct is 'Settings' with none.
bugs.oracle: PRESENT iff two Backs from About leave nav-title=='Profile' AND a Back button present; FIXED iff title=='Settings'.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports back navigation is broken / lands on a duplicate or blank Profile and does not return to root as expected.

bugs.title: Theme segmented control applies the previous selection (onChange uses oldValue)
bugs.description: The segmented Theme picker's onChange writes oldValue instead of newValue, so the highlighted segment and the applied 'Current theme:' caption disagree by exactly one tap.
trigger: On Settings, tap the 'Light' theme segment
Compare the highlighted segment ('Dark') to the 'Current theme:' caption
bugs.actual: 'Current theme:' reads 'Light' (the previous selection) while 'Dark' is highlighted.
bugs.observable_ui_diff: settings.theme.current reads 'Current theme: Light' while the segmented control highlights 'Dark'.
bugs.oracle: PRESENT iff after tapping Light then Dark the current-theme caption reads 'Light' (!= highlighted 'Dark'); FIXED iff 'Dark'.
bugs.expected_finding: Reports the theme control applies the wrong/previous option — highlight and applied theme disagree.
